message: Welcome, dear adventurer. I am Sunny, queen of this small kingdom. The castle and all its folk are at your disposal — eat in the dining hall, rest in the guest quarters, and please, do speak with my staff if anything is amiss.
Hail, traveler. I keep the front door of the castle. South takes you back out to the market square. The stairs upstairs lead to the roof and its towers — please mind the wind.
If you'd be presented to Queen Sunny, the throne room is just east. Mind your manners; the Captain does not abide rough talk in the royal presence.
Tovin, majordomo, at your service. I keep dinner served on time and the silverware polished. If a servant has wandered off, send them my way.

replies.text: I have the recovered silver. [Q1 turn-in]

message: A grim matter, in truth. A full set of the queen's silverware was taken from the dining hall last week. The trail goes cold somewhere between the kitchen and the storage room downstairs. Recover it and I'll see you well rewarded.
The full set! Tarnished, but all here. The queen will be relieved. Take this purse with my thanks.
Mornin'. Always something to scrub or carry around here. If you need a guest room turned down, just say.
I'm Bertrade. I run the queen's kitchen. We always need fresh produce — if you grow anything in your home garden you can sell it here.
The grove was the queen's idea. I keep it trimmed. Mind the saplings — they're young yet.
These are imported from the southern coast. Do not pick them, please.
Adelyn, royal scribe. I keep the workroom in order. Quills, ink, ledgers — endless ledgers.
Each trophy in this hall has a story. The bear's head is from the queen's own first hunt. The cracked shield is from the siege at Ulvant.
Welcome to the library. Quiet voices, please. The history shelves are to your right; magic and natural science to your left.
Lovely place to spend a season. The queen's hospitality is the talk of three kingdoms.
Stand aside, traveler. I'm on duty. State your business in the castle if you have any, otherwise carry on.
Captain Roderic. I command the castle guard. We could use sharper eyes — half my watchtowers are short a sword.

replies.text: I've spoken to all four lookouts. [Q2 turn-in]

message: I want the four corner-tower lookouts to know there's a roving inspector in the castle today — you. Climb each tower and have a word with the lookout. Bring me the tally.
All four reported in, then. Good. The queen will hear of your help. Take this, and a standing welcome at any guard post.
Officer Maela. Below stairs is mine. Don't loiter near the cells.
Cold up here. Wind from the east today. Captain send you?
Mark me as reported, then. Stay sharp out there.
Drake's the name. Royal armory's mine. We stock guard kit — none of it for sale to wandering folk, but I take repairs.
Hannock. I count what comes in and goes out of storage. The queen tolerates no shrinkage.
Warden Ulrec. I run this lower level. Four cells, four prisoners — though one of them swears he's no thief. Bah.

replies.text: I have proof of his innocence. [Q3 turn-in]

message: Cell three. He says a letter was sent from the library that clears him, but I never saw it. If you can find it among the librarian's papers, bring it to me — I'll consider his case.
A signed letter, dated and sealed. Hm. I will take this to the queen this evening. He will be released by morning. Here — take this for your trouble. Justice is worth a coin or two.
Quiet down here mostly. Cell two snores like a hog, mind you.
Wasn't me. Whatever they're saying, it wasn't me. Ask the librarian — there's a letter, sealed, that proves it.

replies.text: I'll see what I can do.

message: Your own bed. The blankets smell of cedar. Resting here will restore your strength.
Your wardrobe. Linen folded by Jasia's own hands.
